explanation: Persea gratissima (avocado) oil is a rich plant oil high in oleic acid (~70%), palmitoleic acid, and lipid-soluble vitamins A, D, and E with excellent emollient and skin-nourishing properties. It is particularly suited for dry and mature skin; its high oleic acid content gives it moderate comedogenic potential on acne-prone or congestion-prone skin at higher concentrations.

Petrolatum (petroleum jelly) is the most effective occlusive agent in skincare — a semi-solid hydrocarbon mixture that reduces transepidermal water loss by nearly 98% and forms a protective barrier on the skin surface. It is considered inert, non-comedogenic, hypoallergenic, and exceptionally well-tolerated — the gold-standard ingredient for barrier repair, wound healing, and protecting compromised or post-procedure skin.

Phaseolus angularis (adzuki bean) seed powder is a finely milled plant powder used in cleansers and exfoliating formulas as a gentle physical exfoliant and absorbent. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, providing mild buffing and oil-absorbing properties suitable for most skin types.

Phaseolus radiatus (mung bean) seed extract provides polyphenol antioxidants, isoflavones, and skin-conditioning compounds with free-radical-scavenging and brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its antioxidant content.

Phenethyl alcohol is an aromatic alcohol found in rose and many other essential oils, used as both a fragrance compound and mild preservative. It is a documented contact sensitizer — particularly in leave-on products — capable of triggering allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin.

Phenoxyethanol is the most widely used synthetic preservative in modern skincare, effectively inhibiting gram-negative bacteria and fungi at pH-independent concentrations. It is generally well-tolerated at approved concentrations (up to 1% in the EU), with a substantially better safety and sensitization profile than parabens, formaldehyde-releasers, and isothiazolinones.

Phenyl trimethicone is a phenyl-modified silicone with a higher refractive index than standard dimethicone, used in foundations and skincare to impart a glossy, luminous finish with optical clarity and light-reflecting properties. It is considered well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and provides excellent spreadability and a luxurious skin feel.

Phenylalanine is an essential aromatic amino acid and component of the skin's natural moisturizing factor, used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent that supports moisture retention. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Phenyltrimethicone is a phenyl-modified silicone with a higher refractive index than standard dimethicone, used in foundations and skincare to impart a glossy, luminous finish with light-reflecting properties. It is considered well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and provides excellent spreadability and a luxurious skin feel.

Phytantriol is a branched-chain aliphatic triol found naturally in chlorophyll, used in skincare as a mild humectant, skin-conditioning agent, and preservative booster with moisture-binding properties. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in hydrating and natural-positioned formulas for its gentle moisturizing and formula-stability contributions.

Phytonadione (vitamin K1) is used in topical skincare for its ability to support capillary health and reduce the appearance of bruising, dark circles, spider veins, and post-procedure redness by supporting blood coagulation and vascular integrity at the skin surface. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in eye area and post-procedure formulas for its vascular-strengthening properties.

Phytosphingosine is a natural sphingoid base found in the skin's lipid barrier that functions as both a ceramide precursor and a direct antimicrobial active against Cutibacterium acnes, with additional anti-inflammatory and barrier-repairing properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — particularly on sensitive, atopic, and acne-prone skin — and used in barrier-repair, calming, and blemish-targeting formulas for its dual barrier-building and antimicrobial activity.

Phytosterols are plant-derived sterols (beta-sitosterol, campesterol, stigmasterol) that structurally resemble human skin cholesterol and integrate into the skin's lipid bilayer to reinforce barrier integrity, reduce inflammation, and improve moisture retention. They are exceptionally well-tolerated and used in barrier-repair and protective formulas for their skin-biomimetic cholesterol-analogue properties.

Phytosteryl oleate is an ester of phytosterols and oleic acid used as a skin-biomimetic emollient that integrates into the skin's lipid barrier structure to provide conditioning and barrier-reinforcing properties. It is well-tolerated and used in barrier-repair and nourishing formulas for its skin-compatible lipid profile.

Phytosteryl/isostearyl/cetyl/stearyl/behenyl dimer dilinoleate is a complex polyfunctional ester combining phytosterols and multiple fatty acid components to provide a rich, skin-compatible emollient with barrier-supportive lipid analogues. It is well-tolerated and used in barrier-repair and moisturizing formulas for its multi-component skin-mimetic lipid profile.

Pinus palustris (longleaf pine) leaf extract provides proanthocyanidin, terpenoid, and polyphenol antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective formulas for its antioxidant content.

Pinus pinaster (maritime pine) bark extract is the botanical source of Pycnogenol — one of the most potent plant antioxidants studied, rich in oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) with exceptional free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and collagen-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its documented antioxidant and skin-firming activity.

Pinus strobus (eastern white pine) bark extract provides proanthocyanidin and polyphenol antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and anti-aging formulas for its antioxidant content.

Pogostemon cablin (patchouli) oil is an essential oil with a characteristic earthy-musky fragrance that carries documented contact sensitization risk due to its sesquiterpene and patchoulol content. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly in leave-on products.

Poloxamine 1307 is a block copolymer surfactant (tetronic-type) used in skincare formulas as a mild solubilizer, emulsifier, and wetting agent. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a formulation aid rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Poly C10-30 alkyl acrylate is a long-chain acrylate polymer used as a rheology modifier and film-former in skincare and sunscreen formulas, providing viscosity and a water-resistant, elegant skin feel. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, functioning as a texture and stability modifier.

Polyacrylate crosspolymer-6 is a crosslinked acrylate polymer used as a thickener and sensory modifier in water-based skincare formulas, providing a lightweight gel texture without the heavy or sticky feel of traditional thickeners. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Polyacrylate-11 is a synthetic acrylate polymer used as a film-former and rheology modifier in skincare formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, functioning as a texture and stability modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Polydiethyleneglycol adipate/IPDI copolymer is a polyester-polyurethane film-former used in long-wear cosmetic formulas to provide durable, flexible coverage. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning as a wear-enhancing texture modifier.

Polydimethylsiloxane/polymethylsilsesquioxane copolymer is a hybrid silicone combining the smoothing properties of dimethicone with the film-forming and soft-focus properties of silsesquioxane resin, used in primers and foundations to blur pores and fine lines. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Polyethylene is a synthetic polymer used in cosmetics in fine powder form as a soft-focus texture agent, slip enhancer, and mild physical exfoliant — though microplastic regulatory concerns have led many brands to replace it with biodegradable alternatives. It is considered inert and non-sensitizing in finished cosmetic formulas.

Polyethylene glycol (PEG) is a generic term for polyethylene glycol polymers used as mild humectants, solubilizers, and co-emulsifiers in skincare formulas. Tolerance varies by molecular weight; low-molecular-weight PEGs are stronger penetration enhancers, while high-molecular-weight forms are milder — individuals with PEG sensitivity should approach any PEG-containing formula with caution.

Polyglyceryl-3 hydroxylauryl ether is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ether emulsifier derived from glycerin and lauric acid, used to create stable emulsions without polyethylene glycol. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and favored in natural and clean-formula systems for its skin-compatible, PEG-free emulsification.

Polyglyceryl-10 laurate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and lauric acid, used to create stable, mild emulsions in natural and clean-formula skincare. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and a popular PEG-free alternative in sensitive-skin formulations.

Polyglyceryl-10 myristate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and myristic acid, used as a mild emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent in natural and clean-formula skincare. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and used in sensitive-skin and natural-positioned emulsions for its skin-compatible, PEG-free emulsification.
